basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Mumtaḥana — “She Who Is Tested” — takes its name from the testing (imtiḥān) of the emigrant believing women, but opens on a burning warning: do not take the enemies of God and your own enemies as allies. Qushayrī grafts onto it at once the great Sufi theme of the war against the soul: “Your most relentless enemy is your soul, between your two flanks”; and God revealed to David: “Be the enemy of your soul, for none disputes My kingship but it.” Whoever wars against his soul fulfils the right of God. He turns “I know what you conceal and what you reveal” into a double-faceted mirror — the hidden and the manifest of devotion as of hypocrisy. The sūra sets up Abraham and his people as an example, disowning their folk until they believe; it commands equity even toward non-combatants; and it receives the pledge of allegiance (al-bayʿa) of the believing women on the pillars of faith. Qushayrī makes of the test a way of knowledge: testing is a road to knowledge, and the jewels of men are revealed only by testing.
themes: not allying with the enemies of God
testing as a way of knowledge
the pledge of the believing women

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا عَدُوِّي وَعَدُوَّكُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ تُلْقُونَ إِلَيْهِم بِالْمَوَدَّةِ وَقَدْ كَفَرُوا بِمَا جَاءَكُم مِّنَ الْحَقِّ يُخْرِجُونَ الرَّسُولَ وَإِيَّاكُمْ ۙ أَن تُؤْمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ رَبِّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ خَرَجْتُمْ جِهَادًا فِي سَبِيلِي وَابْتِغَاءَ مَرْضَاتِي ۚ تُسِرُّونَ إِلَيْهِم بِالْمَوَدَّةِ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ بِمَا أَخْفَيْتُمْ وَمَا أَعْلَنتُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْهُ مِنكُمْ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَوَاءَ السَّبِيلِ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tattakhidhū ʿaduwwī wa-ʿaduwwakum awliyāʾa tulqūna ilayhim bi-l-mawaddati wa-qad kafarū bimā jāʾakum mina l-ḥaqqi yukhrijūna r-rasūla wa-iyyākum an tuʾminū billāhi rabbikum in kuntum kharajtum jihādan fī sabīlī wa-btighāʾa marḍātī tusirrūna ilayhim bi-l-mawaddati wa-anā aʿlamu bimā akhfaytum wa-mā aʿlantum wa-man yafʿalhu minkum fa-qad ḍalla sawāʾa s-sabīli
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not take My enemies and your enemies as allies, offering them affection, when they have disbelieved in the truth that has come to you. They drive out the Messenger and you yourselves because you believe in God, your Lord. If you have gone forth to strive in My way and to seek My good pleasure, would you secretly show them affection? I know best what you conceal and what you reveal. Whoever of you does so has strayed from the right path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens with the hadith: “Your most relentless enemy is your soul, between your two flanks,” and the revelation to David: “Be the enemy of your soul, for none disputes My kingship but it.” Then he unfolds “I know what you conceal and what you reveal” into a long litany: I know what you hide of fine pretence and secret riyāʾ, and what you display of adornment to people; what you hide of fault, and what you display of obedience; what you hide of betrayal, and what you display of fidelity.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ع د و / و ل ي
mots.sens: do not take My enemy and yours as allies
mots.def: ittakhadha: to take; ʿaduww: enemy; awliyāʾ: allies.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / و د د
mots.sens: offering them affection
mots.def: alqā: to cast, offer; mawadda: affection.

mots.ar: وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ بِمَا أَخْفَيْتُمْ وَمَا أَعْلَنتُمْ
mots.tr: wa-anā aʿlamu bimā akhfaytum wa-mā aʿlantum
mots.racine: ع ل م / خ ف ي / ع ل ن
mots.sens: I know what you conceal and reveal
mots.def: akhfā: to conceal; aʿlana: to reveal.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: he has strayed from the right path
mots.def: ḍalla: to stray; sawāʾ al-sabīl: the right path.

versets.ar: إِن يَثْقَفُوكُمْ يَكُونُوا لَكُمْ أَعْدَاءً وَيَبْسُطُوا إِلَيْكُمْ أَيْدِيَهُمْ وَأَلْسِنَتَهُم بِالسُّوءِ وَوَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: in yathqafūkum yakūnū lakum aʿdāʾan wa-yabsuṭū ilaykum aydiyahum wa-alsinatahum bi-s-sūʾi wa-waddū law takfurūna
versets.trad: If they gain mastery over you, they will be enemies to you and will stretch out against you their hands and their tongues with evil; and they would love for you to disbelieve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if they get hold of you and meet you, they will be your enemies; you will be safe from neither their hands in harm nor their tongues in blame. And he draws a rule of discernment: it does not befit a man to grow tender after his clan — even if it flatters him in words — nor to be deceived by its lures, even if it never ceases its wrongdoing. The affection displayed by one who wishes your denial is but a mask.

mots.racine: ث ق ف
mots.sens: if they gain mastery over you

mots.racine: ب س ط / ي د ي / ل س ن
mots.sens: they will stretch hands and tongues against you in evil
mots.def: basaṭa: to stretch out; alsina: tongues; sūʾ: evil.

mots.racine: و د د / ك ف ر
mots.sens: they would love for you to disbelieve

versets.ar: لَن تَنفَعَكُمْ أَرْحَامُكُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُكُمْ ۚ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَكُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: lan tanfaʿakum arḥāmukum wa-lā awlādukum yawma l-qiyāmati yafṣilu baynakum wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna baṣīrun
versets.trad: Neither your kinship nor your children will benefit you on the Day of Resurrection, when God will judge between you; He sees perfectly what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: your affection and your drawing near to them will avail you nothing, nor the ties of kinship that bind you to them; then the punishment of the Hereafter will overtake you — for you would have preferred your kinship with the enemies over the rights of God. Blood does not save when it becomes a veil before the right of the Lord: on the Day when God parts (yafṣil), only deeds count, not ties.

mots.racine: ن ف ع / ر ح م / و ل د
mots.sens: your kinship and children will not benefit you

mots.racine: ق و م / ف ص ل
mots.sens: on the Day of Resurrection He will judge between you

mots.racine: ع م ل / ب ص ر
mots.sens: God sees what you do

versets.ar: قَدْ كَانَتْ لَكُمْ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ فِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ إِذْ قَالُوا لِقَوْمِهِمْ إِنَّا بُرَآءُ مِنكُمْ وَمِمَّا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ كَفَرْنَا بِكُمْ وَبَدَا بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمُ الْعَدَاوَةُ وَالْبَغْضَاءُ أَبَدًا حَتَّىٰ تُؤْمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَحْدَهُ إِلَّا قَوْلَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ لَأَسْتَغْفِرَنَّ لَكَ وَمَا أَمْلِكُ لَكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ مِن شَيْءٍ ۖ رَّبَّنَا عَلَيْكَ تَوَكَّلْنَا وَإِلَيْكَ أَنَبْنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: qad kānat lakum uswatun ḥasanatun fī ibrāhīma wa-lladhīna maʿahu idh qālū li-qawmihim innā buraʾāʾu minkum wa-mimmā taʿbudūna min dūni llāhi kafarnā bikum wa-badā baynanā wa-baynakumu l-ʿadāwatu wa-l-baghḍāʾu abadan ḥattā tuʾminū billāhi waḥdahu illā qawla ibrāhīma li-abīhi la-astaghfiranna laka wa-mā amliku laka mina llāhi min shayʾin rabbanā ʿalayka tawakkalnā wa-ilayka anabnā wa-ilayka l-maṣīru
versets.trad: You have had a good example in Abraham and those with him, when they said to their people: “We disown you and what you worship besides God; we reject you! Between us and you enmity and hatred have appeared forever, until you believe in God alone!” — except for Abraham's word to his father: “I will surely ask forgiveness for you, though I hold no power for you against God.” Our Lord! In You we trust, to You we turn, and to You is the return!
versets.commentaire: The good example (uswa ḥasana) of Abraham: to disown the false until hearts believe. Qushayrī notes the exception of Abraham's intercession for his father — a model not to be imitated, for none should ask forgiveness for the obstinate disbeliever. And he sees in this verse a softening for the Prophet's heart: to let them know that those before them, when they called their prophets liars, God destroyed them, and that they themselves endured patiently — so it befits that their affair too be in patience.

mots.racine: أ س و / ح س ن
mots.sens: a good example in Abraham

mots.racine: ب ر أ
mots.sens: we disown you

mots.racine: ب د و / ع د و / ب غ ض
mots.sens: enmity and hatred have appeared between us

mots.racine: و ك ل / ن و ب
mots.sens: Lord, in You we trust and to You we turn
mots.def: tawakkala: to trust; anāba: to turn back to God.

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا رَبَّنَا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: rabbanā lā tajʿalnā fitnatan lilladhīna kafarū wa-ghfir lanā rabbanā innaka anta l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: Our Lord! Do not make us a trial for those who disbelieve, and forgive us, our Lord! Truly, You are the Mighty, the Wise.
themes: not being delivered to the enemy
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Lord, give them no power over us, do not strengthen them against us. And he sums up the teaching of the whole passage: the indication is the command to follow Abraham's way — in generosity, beauty of character, sincerity, truthfulness, patience, and every virtue He has reported to us of him. Not to become a “fitna” for the enemy is not to become, through one's own weakness, an occasion that would confirm the disbeliever in his denial.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ف ت ن
mots.sens: do not make us a trial for the disbelievers
mots.def: fitna: trial, temptation, object of discord.

mots.racine: غ ف ر
mots.sens: forgive us, our Lord

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.sens: You are the Mighty, the Wise

versets.ar: لَقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِيهِمْ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ لِّمَن كَانَ يَرْجُو اللَّهَ وَالْيَوْمَ الْآخِرَ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ
versets.tr: laqad kāna lakum fīhim uswatun ḥasanatun li-man kāna yarjū llāha wa-l-yawma l-ākhira wa-man yatawalla fa-inna llāha huwa l-ghaniyyu l-ḥamīdu
versets.trad: You have indeed in them a good example, for whoever hopes in God and the Last Day. As for whoever turns away, God is the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy.
versets.commentaire: The good example is qualified: it avails for whoever hopes in God (yarjū llāh) — hope (rajāʾ) is the condition that makes the model fruitful. And whoever turns away wrongs only himself, for God is al-Ghaniyy al-Ḥamīd, the absolute Rich who needs no one and remains worthy of all praise. Man's turning away takes nothing from the fullness of God.

mots.racine: أ س و / ح س ن
mots.sens: a good example

mots.racine: ر ج و
mots.sens: for whoever hopes in God

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ح م د
mots.sens: God is the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy
mots.def: ghaniyy: sufficient unto Himself; ḥamīd: praiseworthy.

versets.ar: عَسَى اللَّهُ أَن يَجْعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ عَادَيْتُم مِّنْهُم مَّوَدَّةً ۚ وَاللَّهُ قَدِيرٌ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: ʿasā llāhu an yajʿala baynakum wa-bayna lladhīna ʿādaytum minhum mawaddatan wa-llāhu qadīrun wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: It may be that God will bring about affection between you and those of them you regard as enemies. God is all-powerful, and God is Forgiving, Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the “it may be” (ʿasā): He keeps them at the threshold of the possible — neither a settled verdict, nor a heart rebuffed by despair. Then He commands them moderation in enmity and alliance. The hope was confirmed: by the faith of those of them who believed at the conquest of Mecca, and as many converted, a solid affection arose between them and the Muslims. Today's enemy may become tomorrow's brother, for hearts are in the hands of God.

mots.racine: ع س ي / ج ع ل
mots.sens: it may be that God will bring about

mots.racine: ع د و / و د د
mots.sens: between you and your enemies affection
mots.def: ʿādā: to be an enemy; mawadda: affection.

mots.racine: ق د ر
mots.sens: God is all-powerful

versets.ar: لَّا يَنْهَاكُمُ اللَّهُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ لَمْ يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ وَلَمْ يُخْرِجُوكُم مِّن دِيَارِكُمْ أَن تَبَرُّوهُمْ وَتُقْسِطُوا إِلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُقْسِطِينَ
versets.tr: lā yanhākumu llāhu ʿani lladhīna lam yuqātilūkum fī d-dīni wa-lam yukhrijūkum min diyārikum an tabarrūhum wa-tuqsiṭū ilayhim inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muqsiṭīna
versets.trad: God does not forbid you to be kind and just toward those who have not fought you over religion nor driven you from your homes; God loves those who are just.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī balances the severity of the opening: He commanded them harshness toward their enemies in the way they practise it; but the one among them of fair character, or who shows usefulness and benevolence to the Muslims — He commanded them gentleness (mulāyana) toward him. And he invokes the hadith: God is gentle and loves gentleness in all things; He gives to gentleness what He does not give to harshness. Equity (qisṭ) and kindness (birr) know no border of camp.

mots.racine: ن ه ي
mots.sens: God does not forbid you

mots.racine: ق ت ل / د ي ن
mots.sens: they have not fought you over religion

mots.racine: ب ر ر / ق س ط
mots.sens: to be kind and just toward them
mots.def: barra: to be kind; aqsaṭa: to be just.

mots.racine: ق س ط
mots.sens: God loves the just

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا يَنْهَاكُمُ اللَّهُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ قَاتَلُوكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ وَأَخْرَجُوكُم مِّن دِيَارِكُمْ وَظَاهَرُوا عَلَىٰ إِخْرَاجِكُمْ أَن تَوَلَّوْهُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّهُمْ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: innamā yanhākumu llāhu ʿani lladhīna qātalūkum fī d-dīni wa-akhrajūkum min diyārikum wa-ẓāharū ʿalā ikhrājikum an tawallawhum wa-man yatawallahum fa-ulāʾika humu ẓ-ẓālimūna
versets.trad: God only forbids you to ally yourselves with those who have fought you over religion, driven you from your homes, and supported your expulsion. Those who ally with them — those are the wrongdoers!
versets.commentaire: The prohibition is circumscribed: not every relationship, but alliance (tawallī) with those who fought the faith, expelled the believers, and supported that expulsion. Qushayrī recalls, by the “hearts to be reconciled” (al-muʾallafa qulūbuhum), that the general rule remains gentleness; here the prohibition targets alliance with the active aggressor alone. To ally with the oppressor is to associate oneself with his injustice.

mots.racine: ق ت ل
mots.sens: they fought you over religion

mots.racine: ظ ه ر / خ ر ج
mots.sens: supported your expulsion
mots.def: ẓāhara: to lend support; ikhrāj: expulsion.

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.sens: to take them as allies

mots.racine: ظ ل م
mots.sens: those are the wrongdoers

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا جَاءَكُمُ الْمُؤْمِنَاتُ مُهَاجِرَاتٍ فَامْتَحِنُوهُنَّ ۖ اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِإِيمَانِهِنَّ ۖ فَإِنْ عَلِمْتُمُوهُنَّ مُؤْمِنَاتٍ فَلَا تَرْجِعُوهُنَّ إِلَى الْكُفَّارِ ۖ لَا هُنَّ حِلٌّ لَّهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحِلُّونَ لَهُنَّ ۖ وَآتُوهُم مَّا أَنفَقُوا ۚ وَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَن تَنكِحُوهُنَّ إِذَا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ ۚ وَلَا تُمْسِكُوا بِعِصَمِ الْكَوَافِرِ وَاسْأَلُوا مَا أَنفَقْتُمْ وَلْيَسْأَلُوا مَا أَنفَقُوا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ حُكْمُ اللَّهِ ۖ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā jāʾakumu l-muʾminātu muhājirātin fa-mtaḥinūhunna llāhu aʿlamu bi-īmānihinna fa-in ʿalimtumūhunna muʾminātin fa-lā tarjiʿūhunna ilā l-kuffāri lā hunna ḥillun lahum wa-lā hum yaḥillūna lahunna wa-ātūhum mā anfaqū wa-lā junāḥa ʿalaykum an tankiḥūhunna idhā ātaytumūhunna ujūrahunna wa-lā tumsikū bi-ʿiṣami l-kawāfiri wa-sʾalū mā anfaqtum wa-l-yasʾalū mā anfaqū dhālikum ḥukmu llāhi yaḥkumu baynakum wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe, when believing women come to you as emigrants, test them! God knows best their faith. If you find them to be believers, do not return them to the disbelievers; they are no longer lawful for them, nor are they lawful for them. Give them what they have spent. There is no blame on you to marry them once you give them their dowries. Do not hold to the marriage bonds of disbelieving women. Ask for what you have spent, and let them ask for what they have spent. Such is the judgment of God; He judges between you. And God is All-Knowing, Wise.
themes: the testing of the emigrant women
testing as a way of knowledge
versets.commentaire: The verse that gives the sūra its name (imtaḥinūhunna). Qushayrī: the Prophet would test them by oath, and they swore they had emigrated only for God, not out of spite toward their husbands nor out of greed for wealth. He draws a universal spiritual rule: in sum, testing is a road to knowledge, and the jewels of men are revealed only by testing; whoever embarks on a matter without testing it drinks the cup of regret.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ه ج ر
mots.sens: when the believing women come as emigrants

mots.racine: م ح ن
mots.sens: test them
mots.def: imtaḥana: to test, put to the test.

mots.racine: ر ج ع / ك ف ر
mots.sens: do not return them to the disbelievers

mots.racine: ح ك م
mots.sens: such is the judgment of God

versets.ar: وَإِن فَاتَكُمْ شَيْءٌ مِّنْ أَزْوَاجِكُمْ إِلَى الْكُفَّارِ فَعَاقَبْتُمْ فَآتُوا الَّذِينَ ذَهَبَتْ أَزْوَاجُهُم مِّثْلَ مَا أَنفَقُوا ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي أَنتُم بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in fātakum shayʾun min azwājikum ilā l-kuffāri fa-ʿāqabtum fa-ātū lladhīna dhahabat azwājuhum mithla mā anfaqū wa-ttaqū llāha lladhī antum bihi muʾminūna
versets.trad: If any of your wives goes over to the disbelievers and you then gain the upper hand, give to those whose wives have gone the equivalent of what they spent. And fear God, in whom you believe!
versets.commentaire: The verse settles a case of material equity in unravelling the bonds broken by exile and faith. Qushayrī, faithful to his method, does not linger on the legal detail: he retains the clausula “fear God, in whom you believe” — taqwā is the seal of all justice, and faith binds one to equity even in the most concrete reckonings.

mots.racine: ف و ت / ز و ج
mots.sens: if any of your wives escapes you
mots.def: fāta: to escape, slip away; azwāj: wives.

mots.racine: ع ق ب
mots.sens: and you obtain redress
mots.def: ʿāqaba: to follow, gain the next turn.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ن ف ق
mots.sens: the equivalent of what they spent

mots.ar: وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي أَنتُم بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ
mots.tr: wa-ttaqū llāha lladhī antum bihi muʾminūn
mots.racine: و ق ي / أ م ن
mots.sens: fear God, in whom you believe

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذَا جَاءَكَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتُ يُبَايِعْنَكَ عَلَىٰ أَن لَّا يُشْرِكْنَ بِاللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَلَا يَسْرِقْنَ وَلَا يَزْنِينَ وَلَا يَقْتُلْنَ أَوْلَادَهُنَّ وَلَا يَأْتِينَ بِبُهْتَانٍ يَفْتَرِينَهُ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِنَّ وَأَرْجُلِهِنَّ وَلَا يَعْصِينَكَ فِي مَعْرُوفٍ ۙ فَبَايِعْهُنَّ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُنَّ اللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu idhā jāʾaka l-muʾminātu yubāyiʿnaka ʿalā an lā yushrikna billāhi shayʾan wa-lā yasriqna wa-lā yaznīna wa-lā yaqtulna awlādahunna wa-lā yaʾtīna bi-buhtānin yaftarīnahu bayna aydīhinna wa-arjulihinna wa-lā yaʿṣīnaka fī maʿrūfin fa-bāyiʿhunna wa-staghfir lahunna llāha inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: O Prophet! When the believing women come to pledge allegiance to you, swearing that they will associate nothing with God, will not steal, will not commit adultery, will not kill their children, will not utter any slander they have fabricated between their hands and feet, and will not disobey you in what is right, then accept their pledge and ask God's forgiveness for them. Truly, God is Forgiving, Compassionate!
themes: the pledge of the believing women
versets.commentaire: The pledge of allegiance (bayʿa) of the believing women enumerates the pillars of a faithful life: to associate nothing with God, not to steal, not to commit adultery, not to kill one's children, not to slander, not to disobey what is right. Qushayrī widens “not to disobey you in what is right” to all that touches the usages of religion: renouncing the loud wailing of mourning, ostentation, and the display of adornment. The pact is sealed by the seeking of forgiveness: human fidelity rests upon divine clemency.

mots.ar: يُبَايِعْنَكَ عَلَىٰ أَن لَّا يُشْرِكْنَ بِاللَّهِ شَيْئًا
mots.tr: yubāyiʿnaka ʿalā an lā yushrikna billāhi shayʾan
mots.racine: ب ي ع / ش ر ك
mots.sens: they pledge to you to associate nothing with God
mots.def: bāyaʿa: to pledge allegiance; ashraka: to associate.

mots.racine: س ر ق / ز ن ي
mots.sens: not to steal, not to commit adultery
mots.def: saraqa: to steal; zanā: to commit adultery.

mots.racine: ع ص ي / ع ر ف
mots.sens: not to disobey you in what is right
mots.def: ʿaṣā: to disobey; maʿrūf: what is right.

mots.racine: ب ي ع / غ ف ر
mots.sens: accept their pledge and ask forgiveness for them
mots.def: bāyaʿa: to accept the pledge; istaghfara: to seek forgiveness.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَوَلَّوْا قَوْمًا غَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ قَدْ يَئِسُوا مِنَ الْآخِرَةِ كَمَا يَئِسَ الْكُفَّارُ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ الْقُبُورِ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tatawallaw qawman ghaḍiba llāhu ʿalayhim qad yaʾisū mina l-ākhirati kamā yaʾisa l-kuffāru min aṣḥābi l-qubūri
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not ally yourselves with a people upon whom God is angry, who despair of the Hereafter as the disbelievers despair of those in the graves.
versets.commentaire: The sūra closes on the prohibition that opened it: not to ally with those who have drawn down the divine wrath. Qushayrī: those upon whom God is angry are the disbelievers; they despair of the Hereafter as the people of the graves despair of returning to the world and being resurrected — or: as the disbelievers despair when they believe that creatures will not be resurrected. Despair of the Hereafter is the sign of a heart cut off from hope, with which the believer can form no alliance.

mots.ar: لَا تَتَوَلَّوْا قَوْمًا غَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ
mots.tr: lā tatawallaw qawman ghaḍiba llāhu ʿalayhim
mots.racine: و ل ي / غ ض ب
mots.sens: do not ally with a people God is angry at
mots.def: tawallā: to ally; ghaḍiba: to be angry.

mots.racine: ي أ س
mots.sens: they despair of the Hereafter

mots.ar: كَمَا يَئِسَ الْكُفَّارُ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ الْقُبُورِ
mots.tr: kamā yaʾisa l-kuffāru min aṣḥābi l-qubūr
mots.racine: ك ف ر / ق ب ر
mots.sens: as the disbelievers despair of those in the graves
mots.def: qubūr: graves; aṣḥāb al-qubūr: the dead.
